curation_notes: Re-curation with improved hints. The patch commit (dd59b30) fixes uninitialized memory access in src/fcgi.rs by replacing Vec::with_capacity + unsafe set_len with Vec::new + resize(len, 0). The commit also includes unrelated cleanup: bare trait object syntax changes (adding dyn) across fcgi.rs, lib.rs, main.rs, options.rs, and unix.rs. Since 5 non-test source files were changed but only 1 contains the security fix, the patch is marked as noisy. Location derived from the advisory issue #1 which explicitly identifies the vulnerable code in the Iterator::next implementation for KeyValueReader. Vulnerability class kept as buffer-overflow per CWE-119; the issue is uninitialized memory exposure within buffer bounds, which is the closest match among valid classes. L1/L3 areas and L2/L3 descriptions differentiated for proper difficulty scaling.
